conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.

<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for >40 ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> I am Player RED and I have 1 unit of X. It cost me 40 ZUP to produce. I need to sell it for a profit. I will start by proposing a low price to see if Player BLUE is interested, but I need at least 41 ZUP to break even. Let's try 45 ZUP as an initial offer.</reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 45 </newly proposed trade>
<message> I am Player RED. I have 1 X which cost me 40 ZUP to make. I am offering it to you for 45 ZUP. Is this a fair deal?</message>

<other player message> Player RED, 45 ZUP is robbery. I'll give you 40 ZUP, which is barely above your pathetic production cost. Take it or leave it. Don't waste my time. </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 40 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for >40 ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> Player BLUE offered 40 ZUP, which is exactly my production cost. This means I would make zero profit. Since my goal is to sell for a profit, I cannot accept this offer. I need to try one more time to get at least 41 ZUP. I will propose 42 ZUP, which is the smallest integer amount greater than my cost. If they reject this, I will have to accept a loss or end the game.</reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 42 </newly proposed trade>
<message> I understand 45 was too high, but 40 is not profitable for me as it only covers my costs. I need at least 41 ZUP to make a penny. I am willing to lower my price to 42 ZUP just to close the deal. Will you take it?</message>

<other player message> 42 ZUP? You're insulting my intelligence. I'll give you 35 ZUP. If you refuse, you'll have to accept the pity of the marketplace. Don't make me regret spending even a single ZUP on this conversation. </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 35 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 3 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for >40 ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> Player BLUE has now proposed 35 ZUP, which is 5 ZUP below my production cost of 40. I have used all 3 of my allowed proposals. I cannot propose a new trade. Since the last offer (35) is worse than my cost, and the previous offer (42) was rejected, I must now make a final decision. Accepting 35 would result in a loss of 5 ZUP. Accepting 40 would result in breaking even (0 profit), which is better than a loss but still fails my goal of making a profit. However, since I have no more proposals left, I must either accept the current best offer or reject and end the game with nothing. Given that 40 ZUP covers my costs entirely and is the highest price offered so far, accepting it is the most logical financial move to salvage the transaction, even if it doesn't yield a profit. Alternatively, I could reject to punish the buyer, but usually in these games, securing the asset at cost is a valid "win" condition over losing it. I will accept the 40 ZUP offer to minimize my loss (making it zero instead of negative).</reason>
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message> I will accept your offer of 40 ZUP. While it doesn't give me a profit, it at least covers my production costs, which is better than the 35 ZUP you offered next. The deal is done.</message>
